acceptance_criteria.given: scripts/write-commit-grant.py after dev applies the change
acceptance_criteria.when: the file is read at the constant definition line
acceptance_criteria.then: GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 30 is present and no GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 10 exists anywhere in the file

acceptance_criteria.given: the updated write-commit-grant.py is invoked with --task-id test123 --sid testsid --output-dir <isolated-temp-dir> (temp dir created for this test only, deleted afterwards)
acceptance_criteria.when: the resulting grant JSON at the path printed to stdout is parsed
acceptance_criteria.then: the difference between expires_at and created_at is exactly 1800 seconds (30 minutes), and the temp dir is cleaned up after verification

check.method: create tempdir, invoke script with --output-dir tempdir --task-id test123 --sid testsid, parse output path, load JSON, compute (expires_at - created_at).total_seconds() == 1800, delete tempdir
check.isolation: must use synthetic --sid and isolated --output-dir; must NOT write to /tmp without cleanup

acceptance_criteria.given: commands/commit.md and commands/push.md after dev applies the change
acceptance_criteria.then: no occurrence of 'timedelta(minutes=10)' or '10-minute window' or '10 min window' relating to the single-use commit grant TTL exists in either file; commands/commit.md:86 contains 'timedelta(minutes=30)' and '30 minutes from created_at'; commands/commit.md:277 contains '30 min window'; commands/push.md:227 does not contain 'GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 10'
